results.label: Confident — GDPR hiring AI, EU
results.input: We are building an AI tool that screens job applicants and ranks them by suitability. We operate in Germany and France.

domains.reasoning: Applicant screening necessarily processes personal data (privacy domain) and explicitly uses AI to rank candidates.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Operations explicitly stated in Germany and France, both EU member states.

data_types.reasoning: Job applicant screening tools inherently process behavioural data (work history, qualifications, performance indicators, etc.).

sector.reasoning: Explicitly described as an applicant screening and ranking tool.

results.label: Confident — US health app
results.input: Our iOS app lets patients in the United States track chronic conditions, log medications, and share data with their doctors.

domains.reasoning: The app processes personal health data (chronic conditions, medications) and transmits it via network (sharing with doctors), requiring privacy and cybersecurity controls.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated as serving patients in the United States; HIPAA applies.

data_types.reasoning: Tracking chronic conditions and medications constitutes health data.

sector.reasoning: An app enabling patients to manage health conditions and communicate with doctors is a healthcare tool.

results.label: Confident — UK fintech credit scoring
results.input: We provide algorithmic credit scoring to UK banks. Our model uses transaction history and behavioural signals to approve or decline loan applications.

domains.reasoning: Credit scoring necessarily processes personal data (privacy), uses algorithmic ML (ai), and bank integrations imply network/API exposure (cyber).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated as serving UK banks.

data_types.reasoning: Transaction history is financial data; behavioural signals and loan decision context explicitly stated.

sector.reasoning: Credit scoring for banks is a core financial services function.

results.label: Confident — children's education platform, US
results.input: We run an online learning platform for K-12 students in the United States. We collect names, grades, usage data, and video recordings of tutoring sessions.

domains.reasoning: System processes personal data including names, grades, usage data, and video recordings of minors.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Platform explicitly serves K-12 students in the United States, triggering federal (FERPA, COPPA) and state education/privacy laws.

data_types.reasoning: K-12 users are minors (children); usage data and grades are behavioural; names and video recordings are general personal information.

sector.reasoning: Platform is explicitly described as an online learning platform serving K-12 students.

results.label: Confident — EU smart city surveillance
results.input: Our computer vision platform processes CCTV footage across public spaces in Amsterdam and Brussels to monitor crowd density and detect incidents in real time.

domains.reasoning: CCTV footage is personal data (privacy); computer vision is AI; real-time monitoring platform has network/API exposure (cyber).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Deployment explicitly stated in Amsterdam and Brussels, both EU jurisdictions subject to GDPR.

data_types.reasoning: CCTV footage of public spaces captures faces (biometric), movement patterns (location, behavioural), and incident detection implies behavioural inference.

sector.reasoning: Public space monitoring and incident detection suggest government/municipal deployment, though could also be transport or retail if privately operated; government is most likely.

results.label: Edge — robotics + public space + EU
results.input: We deploy delivery robots on public streets in Paris and Amsterdam. They use cameras to navigate and avoid pedestrians.

domains.reasoning: Cameras processing pedestrian data in public spaces constitute personal data collection (privacy); networked robots with navigation systems require cyber security controls.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Deployment explicitly stated in Paris (France) and Amsterdam (Netherlands), both EU members.

data_types.reasoning: Camera-based navigation captures pedestrian location and visual features (biometric) on public streets.

sector.reasoning: Delivery robots operating on public streets are a transport/logistics application.
